content_html:
png-cmp: like cmp for PNGs

png-cmp is a program I built that checks if two PNGs are visually equivalent. It’s inspired by the

. Here’s how you use it:

cmp
command
png-cmp a.png b.png

Like 

cmp
, it silently exits if the images are identical, and gives an error if they’re different.

Unlike 

cmp
, it checks pixel data, not binary data. PNGs can look the same but be stored differently. For example, 
png-cmp
ignores text metadata.

I was recently doing an experiment where I wanted to check if two PNGs were visually identical, so I built a tool for it!
content_text: png-cmp: like cmp for PNGs png-cmp is a program I built that checks if two PNGs are visually equivalent. It’s inspired by the cmp command. Here’s how you use it: png-cmp a.png b.png Like cmp , it silently exits if the images are identical, and gives an error if they’re different. Unlike cmp , it checks pixel data, not binary data. PNGs can look the same but be stored differently. For example, png-cmp ignores text metadata. I was recently doing an experiment where I wanted to check if two PNGs were visually identical, so I built a tool for it!
